title: Sporadic Golang runtime panics on syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 and syn-nodejs-puppeteer-12.0
symptoms: Panics occur multiple times per hour
Issue started after upgrading to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0
Downgrading to version 10 resolves the issue
root_cause:
The syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 runtime contains a bug that causes sporadic Golang process panics during canary execution. This is a known runtime defect that affects canaries running frequent checks (e.g., every 5 minutes).

The issue manifests as:
1. Intermittent Golang runtime panics
2. Unexpected process termination
3. Missing data points in availability monitoring
4. Canary execution failures without clear error messages

This commonly occurs with:
- Canaries running on syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 runtime
- High-frequency canary schedules (every 5 minutes or less)
- Canaries that perform multiple endpoint checks

The root cause is a runtime bug that was identified and fixed in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-13.0.

recommendations.title: Upgrade to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-13.0 or later
recommendations.problem: Runtime bug in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 causes sporadic panics

solution.description: Verify the canary is using syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or syn-nodejs-puppeteer-12.0
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Check "Configuration" tab for Runtime version

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics get-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region>
solution.expected_outcome: Confirms canary is using syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or syn-nodejs-puppeteer-12.0

solution.description: Upgrade runtime to version with the panic fix
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click "Actions" > "Edit"
# 4. Update runtime version to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-13.0 or later
# 5. Save changes

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics update-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --runtime-version syn-nodejs-puppeteer-13.0
solution.expected_outcome: Canary updated to new runtime version

solution.description: Verify panics no longer occur after upgrade
solution.command:
# Monitor canary runs for at least 24 hours
aws synthetics get-canary-runs \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --max-results 20
solution.expected_outcome: No panics observed, canary runs consistently without gaps

recommendations.title: Temporary workaround: Downgrade to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0
recommendations.problem: Need immediate mitigation while planning upgrade to version 13

solution.description: Temporarily use version 10 to stop panics

solution.step: Test upgrade using dry run feature
solution.description: Use dry run to safely test syn-nodejs-puppeteer-13.0 before production upgrade
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click "Actions" > "Dry run"
# 4. Select syn-nodejs-puppeteer-13.0 as the runtime version
# 5. Review dry run results before applying to production

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics start-canary-dry-run \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --runtime-version syn-nodejs-puppeteer-13.0
solution.expected_outcome: Dry run completes successfully, confirming compatibility with version 13

documentation_links.title: Performing Safe Canary Upgrades with Dry Run
